response.justification: Maria Rodriguez's background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and full-stack TypeScript/Node.js development, with minimal C++ exposure limited to roughly six months at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project — far short of the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development required. She has no demonstrated fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking systems experience, and her database background skews toward MongoDB and MySQL rather than the mandatory SQL Server or Oracle. A brief stint at RIDE Capital involved investment-related product work but used a Node/TypeScript/GraphQL stack, which does not satisfy the domain-plus-C++ pairing the role demands.
strengths: Demonstrated ability to learn and operate across a wide range of languages and environments, including some C/C++ at National Instruments
Exposure to a financial-services product context at RIDE Capital, indicating familiarity with regulated-industry software delivery practices
Strong communication and knowledge-sharing track record through conference talks, workshops, and open-source community contributions
concerns: C++ experience is approximately six months of professional use versus the 5–10 years explicitly required; no evidence of Windows-platform C++ work
No fixed income, derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking systems domain knowledge — the single required domain qualifier is not met
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; primary database tooling is MongoDB and MySQL, which do not satisfy the mandatory SQL requirement

key_factors.factor: Capital markets / fixed income domain gap
